channel.title: The Grant Desk - next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: next 30 days.

item.title: GovAI Research Fellow (AI Governance) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI)

Location: London, UK or Washington, DC preferred; open to candidates based elsewhere. UK visa sponsorship available; US sponsorship only in exceptional cases.

Award: Full-time 2-year fixed-term contract, renewable. Salary by experience and location: London typically GBP 85,000-110,000; Washington DC typically USD 110,000-180,000, with additional compensation for higher cost-of-living regions such as the SF Bay Area. Benefits: health, dental and vision insurance; GBP 5,000 (~USD 6,000) annual wellbeing budget; GBP 5,000 (~USD 6,000) annual professional-development budget; ~USD 500/month coworking for remote staff; flexible hours; extended parental leave; 10% employer pension contribution; 33 days paid vacation (including public holidays). No application fee.

Duration: 2-year full-time contract, renewable

Deadline: 17 Aug 2026

Research Fellows play a central role in GovAI's research: experienced researchers who conduct independent, high-quality work with direct relevance to AI governance and policy while contributing to GovAI's intellectual community. NOTE: applications open at the end of July 2026; GovAI does not publish a fixed closing date, so the 17 August 2026 date shown here is just when this listing is set to expire (check the GovAI page for the live status). Work may take the form of academic publications, policy memos, reports or blog posts, alongside mentoring early-career researchers (such as Summer and Winter Fellows), shaping GovAI's research strategy and convening stakeholders. Fellows work under a Senior Research Fellow or leadership-team member but have substantial autonomy over projects, agendas and publication formats. TOPICS include risk management, threat modeling, economics of AI, geopolitics of AI, public policy and technical governance; candidates working on adjacent topics are also welcome. ELIGIBILITY: open to a wide range of academic and professional backgrounds (computer science, political science, public policy, economics, philosophy, law, plus government/industry/civil-society experience); no strict educational requirement, but the strongest candidates typically have multiple years of relevant research or policy experience and a track record of high-quality, impactful work. A shared application also covers a one-year visiting Research Scholar position for those with less experience (apply once for both). PROCESS: written application; a paid remote work test (some workstreams add a research agenda and a second paid test); then a final stage of two interviews and reference checks. Deep expertise in technical governance, frontier safety design, cybersecurity or US policy is especially welcome. Contact recruitment@governance.ai with location or access questions.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: London Short Film Festival 2027: Call for Entries
item.description:
Organisation: London Short Film Festival (LSFF)

Location: London, United Kingdom (festival 22-31 January 2027). Open to international entries.

Award: Festival screening, including experimental and artists' moving image strands. Tiered FilmFreeway entry fee (lower for films under 5 minutes); exact amounts on FilmFreeway.

Duration: Festival 22-31 January 2027. Films under 45 minutes completed after 1 January 2025.

Deadline: 18 Aug 2026

The London Short Film Festival (24th edition) accepts short films of all genres under 45 minutes, with dedicated experimental and artists' moving image strands and an international category. Deadline reported as 18 August 2026; confirm exact tier dates and fees on FilmFreeway.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ZAMEK Artist Residency 2027 (ZAMEK Culture Centre, Poznan)
item.description:
Organisation: ZAMEK Culture Centre (Centrum Kultury ZAMEK)

Location: Poznan, Poland

Award: Accommodation in an apartment in the former imperial residence, studio access, a fee of PLN 6,000 gross per month (contract for specific work), a production budget of PLN 5,000 to 15,000 gross depending on length of stay and project, plus curatorial support and publicity. No application fee listed.

Duration: 4 to 8 weeks during 2027 (Conceptual or Operational Residency format)

Deadline: 27 Jul 2026

Open call for ZAMEK Culture Centre's 2027 Artist-in-Residence programme in Poznan. Supports artists working across visual arts, performance, choreography, dance, music, sound art, architecture and filmmaking (literature is excluded). Two formats: a Conceptual Residency (creative stay, no requirement to complete or present a final work, with public meetings at least every two weeks) and an Operational Residency (concluding with an artistic event such as an exhibition, concert or screening, plus regular public meetings). Selected artists receive accommodation in the former imperial residence, a studio, a fee of PLN 6,000 gross per month, a production budget of PLN 5,000 to 15,000 gross, curatorial support and publicity. Residencies run 4 to 8 weeks in 2027. ZAMEK is particularly interested in projects that engage changing realities, the current moment and the future (climate and migration crises, the war in Ukraine, social and political issues) or the context, history and architecture of the Castle; project scale must fit the programme budget, and projects should not rely on external co-financing. Download the Terms & Conditions on the ZAMEK site to apply. Deadline: 27 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: APOSTAXIS Artist Residency & Exhibition 2026 (Ukraine / Cyprus)
item.description:
Organisation: ZMINA (Ukraine) with Cypriot partner organisation

Location: Ternopil, Ukraine and Larnaca, Cyprus (hybrid participation possible)

Award: Fully funded: artist fee of EUR 1,800 (including tax; paid in equivalent UAH for Ukrainian artists), EUR 300 production budget, plus travel, accommodation, meals and curatorial support. No application fee.

Duration: 20-day residency, 6 to 28 October 2026, with a group exhibition in Larnaca in January 2027

Deadline: 26 Jul 2026

APOSTAXIS is an international residency and exhibition programme exploring displacement, memory, identity and cultural resilience. It selects four visual artists (three from Ukraine and one from Cyprus) for two simultaneous 20-day residencies in Ternopil, Ukraine and Larnaca, Cyprus (6 to 28 October 2026), followed by post-production, a digital exhibition, and a curated physical exhibition in Larnaca in January 2027. ELIGIBILITY: visual artists from Ukraine and Cyprus with lived experience or family heritage connected to displacement, working in community-engaged or research-based practices (installation, mixed media, participatory, social practice, documentary, performance, archive-based work). Artists unable to travel due to war-related restrictions or personal circumstances remain eligible via hybrid participation. Fully funded: EUR 1,800 artist fee (equivalent UAH for Ukrainian artists), EUR 300 production budget, travel, accommodation, meals and curatorial support; no application fee. Deadline: 26 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Southern California Drone Film Festival 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Southern California Drone Film Festival

Location: San Diego area, California, United States (festival 2-3 October 2026). Open worldwide, 16+.

Award: Trophies, DJI gear (Mini 4 Pro, Osmo Action), Adorama gift cards, and a cash grand prize across 12 categories including News/Documentary, Cinematic FPV, Single Take, 360/VR, and student photo/video. FilmFreeway entry fee applies.

Duration: Festival 2-3 October 2026. Films max 5 minutes (documentaries 10 minutes), at least 50 percent drone footage.

Deadline: 17 Aug 2026

Drone film competition open worldwide to filmmakers aged 16+. Films must be at most 5 minutes (documentaries up to 10) with at least half the footage captured by drone; 12 categories including News/Documentary, Cinematic FPV, Single Take, and 360/VR. Regular deadline 27 July 2026; late deadline 17 August 2026. Entry via FilmFreeway.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FUJIFILM GFX Challenge Grant Program 2026
item.description:
Organisation: FUJIFILM

Location: Open to residents of the countries/regions listed under Entry Submission on the program page. Final content showcased on fujifilm-x.com plus planned exhibitions in various countries.

Award: 5 Global Grant Awards of USD 10,000 and 10 Regional Grant Awards of USD 5,000. All recipients also get complimentary use of a GFX System camera body (including the FUJIFILM GFX ETERNA 55 filmmaking camera) and two GF lenses for the duration of the project, plus mentoring and technical assistance from Fujifilm technicians and product experts. Total approximate retail value of all awards: USD 229,500. No purchase necessary; no entry fee.

Duration: Application window 16 June to 17 August 2026. Production period concludes in May 2027, after which final content is showcased on fujifilm-x.com and in a planned series of exhibitions.

Deadline: 17 Aug 2026

The GFX Challenge Grant Program, sponsored by FUJIFILM and running since 2021, awards 5 Global Grant Awards (USD 10,000) and 10 Regional Grant Awards (USD 5,000) to help aspiring creatives bring imaging projects to life. It is designed to nurture emerging and promising content creators, giving them the opportunity to create content on topics with significant meaning to them while gaining experience with FUJIFILM GFX System gear, including the GFX ETERNA 55 filmmaking camera. Proposed projects may be submitted as still photography or in movie format. AWARDS: both tiers include complimentary use of a GFX System camera body and two GF lenses for the duration of the project, plus mentoring and technical assistance from Fujifilm technicians and product experts. At the end of the production period in May 2027, recipients' final content is showcased on fujifilm-x.com, with a series of exhibitions planned in various countries. ELIGIBILITY: open to both amateur and professional photographers and videographers who are residents of the countries/regions listed in the Entry Submission section of the program page; not open to employees or officers of FUJIFILM and its subsidiaries. TO APPLY: combine three items into a single PDF: (1) the preformatted cover letter (downloadable from the program page); (2) the project proposal covering descriptive plan, objectives, evaluation and outcome measures, communications, how you will use the Fujifilm partnership and mentoring, schedule, and budget; (3) a portfolio for each individual involved. Proposals must be in English (optionally accompanied by a version in your own language; language proficiency is not judged). Entry period 16 June to 17 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: QueerCineMad 2026 (31st edition, formerly LesGaiCineMad)

Organisation: Fundacion Triangulo

Location: Madrid, Spain (festival November 2026). Open internationally.

Award: Screening plus prizes up to EUR 2,500 at a Goya- and Feroz-qualifying festival. Platform submission fees apply (Festhome/FilmFreeway).

Duration: Festival November 2026; notification by 2 November 2026.

Deadline: 15 Aug 2026

QueerCineMad (formerly LesGaiCineMad), organized by Fundacion Triangulo in Madrid, is a Goya- and Feroz-qualifying LGBTI+ film festival offering prizes up to EUR 2,500. International submissions close 15 August 2026 via Festhome or FilmFreeway (platform fees vary).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Adrianna Newell Fellowship at Arts&Rec 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Arts&Rec Inc

Location: Stamford, NY (Western Catskills), USA

Award: Live/work residency: use of a 1000 sq ft studio and attached apartment, plus a $1,500 total materials stipend and per diem. Travel (especially international) is not funded. No application fee.

Duration: October 2026 (four weeks to two months, based on project needs; possible extension into November)

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Inaugural Adrianna Newell Fellowship, a live/work studio residency in Stamford, NY (Western Catskills) for October 2026, with possible extension into November. Named for board member Adrianna Newell, it invites queer lens-based artists working in photography and moving image with an active, research-driven or conceptually rich practice, especially work engaging queer perspectives on land, rurality and regional space, or queer identity, memory, intimacy, community and embodiment. Open to artists from everywhere. Fellows receive a 1000 sq ft studio with attached apartment, a $1,500 combined materials stipend and per diem, and introductions to the local creative community; an open-studio presentation, artist talk or public exhibition at the end is required. Travel, especially international, is not funded (residents are encouraged to seek travel support elsewhere). Apply by emailing a single PDF (one-page description of your work and planned project, 10 to 20 work samples with short descriptions, and a current CV) to programs@artsrec.org. Deadline: 31 July 2026; notices by end of August.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Sarabande Foundation: Studio Residency 2026/27 (UK-based only)
item.description:
Organisation: Sarabande Foundation

Location: London, United Kingdom. Requires a UK address and right to work in the UK; no visa sponsorship.

Award: Heavily subsidised studio space (GBP 1 per sq ft per month including utilities), mentoring from industry experts, pro bono business support, and access to the Sarabande network. Free to apply.

Duration: Residencies start November 2026.

Deadline: 01 Aug 2026

The Sarabande Foundation, founded by Lee Alexander McQueen, offers subsidised London studio residencies for creatives across disciplines (fine art, fashion, design, digital), with structured mentoring and pro bono expert support. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must have a UK address and the right to work in the UK; the foundation cannot sponsor visas. Deadline 1 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

MQFF, Australia's largest queer film festival, accepts international films of interest to LGBTQIA+ audiences. Deadline 3 August 2026; fees USD 30 for shorts and USD 50 for features, with fee waivers reserved for Australian and First Nations entrants.

Open call details

item.title: Night as Commons and the Dark Side of Light: Residency (Transylvania)
item.description:
Organisation: Hypha etc.

Location: Campu Cetatii, Transylvania, Romania. For artists based in Creative Europe countries except Romania (Netherlands eligible).

Award: EUR 30/day allowance, plus accommodation and travel (EUR 400 under 5,000 km; EUR 800 over), with green mobility, family, and accessibility top-ups. EU-funded. 6 weeks. No application fee.

Duration: Residency 1 October to 10 November 2026.

Deadline: 01 Aug 2026

Hypha etc. hosts a six-week EU-funded residency (1 October to 10 November 2026) in Campu Cetatii, Transylvania, on the theme Night as Commons and the Dark Side of Light: night, darkness, and light pollution, a strong fit for media and installation practices. Support: EUR 30/day allowance, accommodation, travel allowance (EUR 400 under 5,000 km, EUR 800 over), plus green mobility, family, and accessibility top-ups. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in Creative Europe countries except Romania. Deadline 1 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Flickr x Black Women Photographers Grant 2026 (Traces of Connection)
item.description:
Organisation: Flickr and Black Women Photographers (BWP)

Location: Open internationally to Black female and non-binary photographers; winner announced at the MODE by Flickr festival in Minneapolis, USA (18-20 September 2026).

Award: USD 10,000 grant to invest in the recipient's practice, plus a two-year Flickr Pro and one-year SmugMug Pro membership; ten additional recipients each receive one-year Flickr Pro and SmugMug Pro memberships. No entry fee (BWP membership is free).

Duration: Winner announced live at MODE by Flickr, 18-20 September 2026

Deadline: 06 Aug 2026

Flickr and Black Women Photographers award a USD 10,000 grant to a Black female or non-binary photographer to invest in their practice. The 2026 edition centers on the theme Traces of Connection, chosen by 2025 recipient and guest judge Eleonore Menga. TO APPLY: be a member of the Black Women Photographers organization (free), join the BWP Flickr Community, submit one photograph inspired by the theme to the community, and share a brief description of how the image connects to it. The winner also receives a two-year Flickr Pro and a one-year SmugMug Pro membership, and is announced live at the first MODE by Flickr festival in Minneapolis (18-20 September 2026); ten additional recipients receive Flickr Pro and SmugMug Pro memberships. DEADLINE: 6 August 2026. No entry fee.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wave Hill / AnkhLave Arts Alliance: 2027 Van Lier Fellowship (NYC)
item.description:
Organisation: Wave Hill / AnkhLave Arts Alliance

Location: Wave Hill Public Garden and Cultural Center, the Bronx, New York City, USA. For NYC-based BIPOC visual artists aged 30 or younger.

Award: $10,435 stipend, including an $8,000 honorarium, a transportation stipend for the residency, and professional development opportunities, plus the Winter Workspace residency, a solo exhibition with public programs, mentorship from Wave Hill curatorial staff and arts professionals, and AnkhLave Alumni Network benefits. Two fellowships awarded. No application fee.

Duration: January to December 2027; Winter Workspace residency 23 February - 18 April 2027; solo exhibition 15 May - 4 July 2027

Deadline: 10 Aug 2026

Wave Hill, in partnership with AnkhLave Arts Alliance, invites NYC-based artists who identify as BIPOC to apply for the 2027 Van Lier Fellowship as part of the 9th Annual AnkhLave Garden Project at Wave Hill Public Garden and Cultural Center in the Bronx. The project supports artists who draw on research, scholarship and lived knowledge to present site-responsive work at the intersection of nature, culture and place, engaging with Wave Hill's plant collections, history, design and architecture. The Van Lier Fellowship (funded by the Edward and Sally Van Lier Fund through the New York Community Trust) will be awarded to two NYC-based visual artists aged 30 or younger. FELLOWSHIP: participation in the Winter Workspace residency (23 February - 18 April 2027); a $10,435 stipend including an $8,000 honorarium and transportation stipend; a solo exhibition in the Sunroom or Sun Porch (15 May - 4 July 2027); public engagement through Winter Workspace programming and a Meet the Artist program; mentorship with Wave Hill curatorial staff, working artists and arts professionals; and AnkhLave Alumni Network benefits. ELIGIBILITY: emerging artists 30 or younger (born 1997 or after) from historically underrepresented communities; live in one of the five NYC boroughs; demonstrate financial need; have completed college and not be enrolled in a degree-granting program during the residency; not represented by a commercial gallery; available for both programs; cannot have been a Van Lier Fellow elsewhere or have previously done a Winter Workspace residency or exhibited at Wave Hill or with AnkhLave. Works may not include nudity, depictions of violence or profanity. Artists may apply to only one of the two 2027 AnkhLave Garden Project opportunities. Questions: visualarts@wavehill.org with '2027 Van Lier-Ankhlave' in the subject. DEADLINE: 10 August 2026. Apply via SlideRoom.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TaDA Textile and Design Alliance: Artist in Residence Programme 2027 (Switzerland)
item.description:
Organisation: TaDA Textile and Design Alliance

Location: Arbon, Eastern Switzerland (Lake Constance). Open to Swiss and international practitioners.

Award: Funded residency: accommodation in Arbon a few minutes' walk from the workplace and the Bodensee, travel expenses, and a contribution to living costs, plus workspace at the Kreativzentrum ZIK (a former textile factory) and technology, knowledge and infrastructure support from 13 partner textile companies and research institutions. No application fee.

Duration: Three months: April to June 2027 or September to November 2027

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

TaDA (Textile and Design Alliance) invites artists, designers, architects, writers, performers and interdisciplinary practitioners to a three-month residency in Arbon, Eastern Switzerland, developing new work through direct collaboration with regional textile companies and research institutions. Applicants do not need to identify as textile artists; the programme welcomes practitioners from multiple disciplines who want to work with textile technology, materials and know-how. Six residents are selected annually by jury for one of two 2027 periods (April-June or September-November). Residents develop a new project in cooperation with local textile companies, present their work publicly and organise workshops. SUPPORT: accommodation, travel expenses, contribution to living costs, workspace at the Kreativzentrum ZIK, and access to the expertise and infrastructure of 13 partner companies. Applicants should identify specific TaDA partners whose expertise contributes directly to the proposal. Apply via the online form. DEADLINE: 12 August 2026, 23:59 CEST; selection results announced early November 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

Location: Krzyzowa, Poland. For residents of Creative Europe countries other than Poland (Netherlands eligible).

Award: Accommodation, financial support, and a production budget (amounts not published). 6-week residency. No application fee.

Duration: Residency 21 September to 31 October 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Krzyzowa Foundation's Konrad and Pawel Jarodzki Artist-in-Residence Programme hosts an artist for six weeks (21 September to 31 October 2026) at the historic Krzyzowa estate in Lower Silesia, providing accommodation, financial support, and a production budget (amounts not published on the call page). ELIGIBILITY: residents of Creative Europe countries other than Poland. Deadline 31 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Cork International Film Festival 2026: Call for Entries
item.description:
Organisation: Cork International Film Festival

Location: Cork, Ireland. Open to international entries; most strands require an Irish premiere.

Award: Screening at Ireland's oldest film festival (November 2026), including Academy Award and BAFTA qualifying shorts strands. FilmFreeway submission fee applies; fee waivers available by emailing info@corkfilmfest.org with the subject WAIVER REQUEST.

Duration: Festival runs November 2026. Features completed after 1 December 2025; shorts after 1 July 2025, max 40 minutes.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Cork International Film Festival, Ireland's oldest film festival, accepts fiction, documentary, animation, and experimental work across features and shorts, with Academy Award and BAFTA qualifying shorts strands. Late deadline for features, feature documentaries, international shorts, and documentary shorts is 31 July 2026 (Irish shorts have until 7 August 2026). Most strands require an Irish premiere. Fee waivers available on request.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CRASSH 25th Anniversary Exhibition: Knowledge in a Fractured World
item.description:
Organisation: CRASSH, University of Cambridge

Location: Cambridge, United Kingdom (Alison Richard Building). Open worldwide.

Award: Exhibition from 5 October to 11 December 2026; no commission taken on sales. Free to enter.

Duration: Exhibition 5 October to 11 December 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Centre for Research in the Arts, Social Sciences and Humanities (CRASSH) at the University of Cambridge marks its 25th anniversary with an open-call exhibition on the theme Knowledge in a Fractured World: how knowledge is produced, contested, and shared in a fragmented age. Accepts 2D, 3D, video, film, and sound work; a strong thematic fit for critical data and research-based practices. Open worldwide, free to enter, no commission on sales. Deadline 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Bloomberg Green Docs Competition 2026 (Short Climate Documentary)
item.description:
Organisation: Bloomberg Green

Location: Open call for filmmakers (see eligibility rules on the site); festival screening in New York City, USA.

Award: $25,000 grand prize awarded by a jury; the top five documentaries screen at the Bloomberg Green Docs Film Festival in New York City. No entry fee stated.

Duration: Winner announced at the festival on 22 September 2026

Deadline: 14 Aug 2026

The Bloomberg Green Docs competition is open to all eligible filmmakers competing for a $25,000 grand prize for a short climate documentary. The call seeks films that explore our climate future and reveal the world we are making today: ecosystems being restored, tomorrow's zero-carbon communities being formed, and habitat extremes warning of cascading climate consequences. REQUIREMENTS: films must address the urgency of climate change and run under 10 minutes including credits. The jury awards a $25,000 grand prize, announced at the Bloomberg Green Docs Film Festival in New York City on 22 September 2026, where the top five documentaries will be screened. Check the official rules on the competition site for eligibility details. DEADLINE: submissions accepted through 14 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Corridor: DAISY CHAIN Artist Commission (Adur, West Sussex)
item.description:
Organisation: Corridor Projects

Location: Adur and Worthing, West Sussex, UK. Open to a socially-engaged artist based in the South East of England working with moving image / lens-based media.

Award: GBP 4,200 artist fee (inclusive of VAT) paid in instalments, plus travel, and an additional budget for materials for creative production, workshops and exhibition outputs (e.g. print). No application fee.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027: workshops September-January, post-production to February 2027, UK and Czech tour March-July 2027

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

Corridor seeks a socially-engaged artist based in the South East of England working with moving image / lens-based media for DAISY CHAIN, marking the 60th anniversary of Vera Chytilova's experimental film Daisies (1966). The commissioned artist will work with a group of LGBTQIA+ young people aged 15-26 in Adur (West Sussex) to co-create new artworks: a 2-3 minute moving image work and a series of 2D collage or mixed media artworks for public exhibition and screenings. DAISY CHAIN commissions six new artist moving image works overall (three England-based artists working with older women, young women and LGBTQIA+ youth; three artists in Czechia), developed in partnership with the National Film Museum in Prague (NaFilM) and Brighton-based videoclub, with results toured across England and Czechia in 2027. EXPECTATIONS: devise about eight hands-on workshops incorporating film, collage and mixed media (participants won't necessarily have an arts background); participate in 4-6 online peer sessions on collaborative practice led by artist Annis Joslin; complete exhibition-ready artworks on time and to budget; maintain a transparent dialogue on authorship with participants. Artists must have a participatory practice and experience of working creatively with young people. Note: to create a safe space, workshops will not be publicly labelled as LGBTQIA+. FEE: GBP 4,200 incl. VAT plus travel and a materials budget. TIMELINE: shortlist notified 7 August, online interviews 12 August 2026; launch event in Adur/Worthing March 2027. DEADLINE: 10am, 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: A.R.M. Holding / Art Dubai Projects: H Residence Sculpture Park Commission (Dubai)
item.description:
Organisation: A.R.M. Holding / HUNA / Art Dubai Projects

Location: H Residence Sculpture Park, Dubai, UAE. Open to UAE-based, regional and international artists.

Award: AED 1,000,000 commission envelope, inclusive of artist fee, design development, fabrication, materials, production, transport, installation, lighting and finishing. Proposal fees are available for shortlisted artists. No submission fee.

Duration: Proposal development September 2026; fabrication autumn 2026; unveiling 2 December 2026

Deadline: 25 Jul 2026

A.R.M. Holding and HUNA, with the commission supported and managed by Art Dubai Projects, invite artists to propose a permanent public artwork for the H Residence Sculpture Park in Dubai, the anchor piece of the park's programme. Artists respond to the theme 'We Emerge Stronger than Before' through a permanent, site-specific outdoor sculpture; the strongest proposals will find what the phrase opens up (continuity, endurance, renewal, collective presence, the long view) through material, form, scale and the conditions of the site. Where language or text is incorporated, Arabic should lead. TECHNICAL: footprint minimum around 15 sqm, up to 3 m high; weight restriction 5.5 kN/m2; must be suitable for long-term outdoor display in Dubai (summer over 40 C, humidity to 90 percent, about six hours direct sun daily); presence by day and night. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists with or without gallery representation; UAE-based, regional and international artists welcome; prior experience with permanent or large-scale outdoor/public installation required. BUDGET: AED 1,000,000 all-inclusive; shortlisted artists receive full budget parameters and proposal fees at the proposal development stage. SUBMISSION: CV and portfolio (attention to outdoor, large-scale or permanent commissions), concept statement (max 300 words), a render or visualisation in context plus at least one sketch, technical specifications, and a site response. TIMELINE: submissions close 25 July 2026; shortlist notified from 10 August; commission awarded after September proposal development; work unveiled at H Residence on 2 December 2026. Local fabrication strongly preferred. Questions: Shahad@artdubai.ae.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: British Council Ireland: UK-IRL Culture Connects Grant 2026/27
item.description:
Organisation: British Council Ireland / Arts Council Ireland

Location: Ireland and the UK. For bilateral partnerships of one individual based in Ireland and one based in England, Northern Ireland, Scotland or Wales; in-person collaboration required.

Award: Up to EUR 3,000 per collaboration (smaller amounts also eligible), covering artist fees (including your own time), collaborator fees, travel and accommodation, studio or space hire, materials, and access costs including disability support. No application fee.

Duration: Activity between October 2026 and March 2027; must be completed by 31 March 2027

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The UK-IRL Culture Connects Grant 2026/27 is a British Council Ireland initiative delivered in association with Arts Council Ireland, supporting the early stages of artistic collaboration between Ireland and the four UK nations through in-person exchange. It prioritises creative development over final outputs: research and development of new ideas, mentorship, exploring collaborative pathways with producers or partners, experimenting with new technologies or practices, and developing initiatives that support creative exchange. WHO: applications are made by individual artists or creative practitioners working as a bilateral partnership of one individual based in Ireland and one based in England, Northern Ireland, Scotland or Wales; applicants must be resident in Ireland or the UK for the project duration, have a proven professional track record, be at an early or developing career stage, and not be in full-time education. One application per collaboration. FUNDING: up to EUR 3,000 covering artist and collaborator fees, travel and accommodation, studio hire, materials, and access costs. Activities must involve in-person collaboration (fully remote projects are not eligible) and take place between October 2026 and 31 March 2027. Apply via the British Council GoodGrants platform; an information webinar recording and overview document are on the programme page. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Modern Art Oxford: Collaborative Micro-commissions Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Modern Art Oxford

Location: Oxford, UK. Open to collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex).

Award: GBP 1,000 artist fee per selected collective, plus up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship from the Modern Art Oxford team, four months of public exhibition display, professional installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, artwork transport support within the South East, peer networking, and inclusion in a public preview event. No application fee.

Duration: Production August to November 2026; install 23 November to 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027

Deadline: 26 Jul 2026

Modern Art Oxford marks its 60th anniversary with an open call for three collaborative micro-commissions responding to the question: how can collective practice/s help us to embrace uncertainty and complexity? Collectives, duos, partnerships and collaborative groups based across the South East of England are invited to propose new small-scale experimental works for a group exhibition in the Ground Floor Gallery, running alongside a major solo exhibition by Mike Nelson, who is also on the selection panel. Proposals may take the form of multidisciplinary research, site-responsive work, grassroots organising, performance, visual art, sound, poetry or other creative practices; ideas should respond to the theme of collective practice and uncertainty, explore collaborative approaches, experiment across disciplines, be achievable within the budget and timeline, and be original to this open call (building on existing research is fine). WHO: groups of two or more people, including established artist collectives, newly formed collaborations, interdisciplinary partnerships, informal or community-led groups, and family collaborations, based in the Arts Council England South East area (Oxfordshire, Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Hampshire and Isle of Wight, Surrey, Kent, East Sussex, West Sussex). SUPPORT: each of the three selected collectives receives a GBP 1,000 artist fee, up to GBP 500 production budget, curatorial mentorship, four months of exhibition display, installation photography, promotion, technical and production support, and transport support within the South East. TIMELINE: online briefing and Q&A 15 June 2026 (email creativelearning@modernartoxford.org.uk for the link); selection confirmed 14 August 2026; production August to November 2026; preview party 4 December 2026; exhibition 5 December 2026 to April 2027. Apply via Google Forms; an information pack PDF and FAQ are on the call page. DEADLINE: Sunday 26 July 2026, 23:59.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AVISTAVULCÃO: Azores Artist Residency 2027 (Faial, Portugal)
item.description:
Organisation: AVISTAVULCÃO / Barca 13

Location: Faial Island, Azores, Portugal (near the Capelinhos Volcano). Open to artists from countries eligible for the Creative Europe programme.

Award: EUR 1,350 allowance per resident for the month, plus travel allowance (EUR 400 to 800), green mobility top-up (EUR 400), visa top-up (EUR 120/resident), outermost regions and overseas territories top-up (EUR 175/resident), family top-up (EUR 200 per child/resident), and accessibility support. No application fee.

Duration: One month, March 2027

Deadline: 01 Aug 2026

AVISTAVULCÃO opens a call for a one-month artist-in-residence programme focused on research and creation, taking place in March 2027 and intended for an artist or artistic duo. Located on the island of Faial near the Capelinhos Volcano (Azores, Portugal), the residency offers immersion in a territory shaped by geology, insularity, and the interplay between nature and human presence, with the Azores treated as living material for creation. Artists are invited to develop projects engaging with landscape, memory, local history, and the contemporary challenges facing peripheral regions; suggested starting points include geological time, transformation, resilience, isolation, mobility, and the relationships between communities and the environment. Context-sensitive proposals that prioritise observation, listening and a relationship with the place are valued. Residents meet local stakeholders and share moments with the community, culminating in a public presentation of the process or results. SUPPORT (in-person mobility): EUR 1,350 allowance per resident, travel allowance of EUR 400 to 800, green mobility top-up of EUR 400, visa top-up of EUR 120 per resident, outermost regions top-up of EUR 175 per resident, family top-up of EUR 200 per child, and accessibility support. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and duos (cross-disciplinary welcome) from countries eligible for the Creative Europe programme. DEADLINE: 1 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Black Arts Centre: Call for Exhibition and Programming Proposals
item.description:
Organisation: Black Arts Centre

Location: Surrey, British Columbia, Canada. Priority to Black artists, curators, and art organizers, and to projects aligned with the gallery's mandate (BIPOC, Disabled, Queer, Immigrant/Refugee/Newcomer perspectives).

Award: Successful proposals receive financial, budgeting, and curatorial support from the Black Arts Centre, including project execution, production, logistics, and marketing. No submission fee.

Duration: Exhibition proposals considered for Spring 2027 and beyond; programming proposals for projects taking place from September 2026 onward. Submission portal open 1 June to 8 August 2026; applicants begin to hear back in September.

Deadline: 08 Aug 2026

The Black Arts Centre is calling for proposals from artists for exhibitions and public programming. Exhibition proposals are considered for Spring 2027 and beyond, while programming proposals are considered for projects taking place from September 2026 onward. The submission portal is open from 1 June to 8 August 2026. Proposals are reviewed by the Black Arts Centre team and a revolving committee of external community members, evaluated using an internal rubric; applicants begin to hear back in September. SUPPORT: successful proposals receive financial, budgeting, and curatorial support from the Black Arts Centre, including project execution, production, logistics, and marketing. ELIGIBILITY AND PRIORITY: applicants do not need to identify as Black to submit, but priority is given to Black artists, curators, and art organizers, and to artists and projects that align with the gallery's mandate (BIPOC, Disabled, Queer, Immigrant/Refugee/Newcomer perspectives). Submission does not guarantee approval for this cycle, but all proposals are kept on file for future consideration, and updated or alternate proposals are accepted. Questions and PDF proposals/portfolios: info@theblackartscentre.ca. Deadline 8 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Railyard Art Project: Call for Temporary Public Art (Santa Fe)
item.description:
Organisation: Railyard Art Project (Railyard Park + Plaza)

Location: Santa Fe, New Mexico, United States (Railyard Park + Plaza, City-owned property under a Conservation Easement).

Award: USD 3,500 honorarium per project (two projects selected), plus marketing support and liability insurance. Funded by Santa Fe's Arts and Culture Commission. No application fee.

Duration: Two projects hosted between October 2026 and June 2027.

Deadline: 05 Aug 2026

The Railyard Art Project (RAP) facilitates temporary public art installations by established and emerging artists and arts collectives in Santa Fe's Railyard Park + Plaza. RAP is accepting proposals for temporary public art installations and creative projects; thanks to funding from Santa Fe's Arts and Culture Commission, two projects will be hosted between October 2026 and June 2027, each receiving a USD 3,500 honorarium plus marketing support and liability insurance. THEMES: the RAP Committee encourages collaboration across disciplines (art, science, history) and welcomes thoughtful, challenging, playful, and edifying art in the context of social and cultural expression and environmental sustainability. Proposed projects must physically withstand outdoor public display and engage the community by responding to the natural and social landscape of the park. SELECTION CRITERIA: overall artistic merit; context and familiarity with the proposed site; practical viability; and potential for community engagement and cultural/educational enrichment. Artists should select an eligible area of the park's open space and describe the project in detail; photos or renderings may be uploaded with the application (a map of Railyard Art Locations is linked from the program page). The RAP Committee, made up of community members and Park + Plaza stakeholders, selects and schedules the exhibitions; a formal in-person presentation may be requested depending on complexity. CONDITIONS: the park operates under a Conservation Easement, so installations cannot be located on planted areas, no staking of any kind is permitted (including during install/de-install), and nothing may be hung from trees. Artists lend artwork at their own risk (no protection against vandalism or weather damage, and artists are responsible for repairs). Installs and de-installs must be coordinated in advance with the RAP Committee or Railyard Park Conservancy staff, with a Committee or staff member on site throughout. Application deadline 5 August 2026.

Open call details

The Arts Council of Ireland's Visual Arts Project Award funds professional visual artists, curators, and producers to develop and deliver visual arts projects, in three strands: planning and pre-production (up to EUR 12,000), production and engagement (up to EUR 60,000), and partnership production (up to EUR 150,000). Primarily aimed at applicants based in the Republic of Ireland, but the Arts Council's standard eligibility clause allows applicants based elsewhere where the application explicitly demonstrates how the outcomes will benefit the arts in Ireland. The wider Project Award 2027 covers nine disciplines including film and music on a similar timeline. Deadline 23 July 2026, 17:30 Irish time.

item.title: Akademie Schloss Solitude: Web Residencies No. 22 - Ignore All Previous Instructions
item.description:
Organisation: Akademie Schloss Solitude

Location: Online (fully remote). Awarded by Akademie Schloss Solitude, Stuttgart, Germany.

Award: EUR 1,200 grant for a four-week online residency, plus a microsite to develop and publish the project, and support from a web/UX designer and a programmer in a supportive capacity.

Duration: Four-week online residency.

Deadline: 07 Aug 2026

Web Residencies No. 22, 'Ignore All Previous Instructions,' is curated by !Mediengruppe Bitnik as part of Akademie Schloss Solitude's Web Residencies program, which since 2016 has supported young talents from the international digital scene as well as artists of all disciplines engaging with web-based practices. For the tenth anniversary of the program, this edition invites artists to create works that challenge extractive digital platforms and AI systems, exploring how to interrupt dominant technological logics, from ad-tech surveillance to prompt injection vulnerabilities, and imagine alternative structures and communication methods. The open call seeks proposals that hijack, glitch, and disrupt rather than comply with existing systems, drawing on strategies like Situationist detournement to map routes through and around the grids we are trapped within. Four to six proposals will be selected. BENEFITS: a four-week online residency; a grant of EUR 1,200; a web space (microsite) on which the project can be developed and published online; support from a web/UX designer and a programmer (in a supportive capacity only, not to build an entire website); and participation in individual and group online events with the curators and Solitude fellows. Offline or hybrid formats (a reading session, panel, exhibition, or curated program) may follow. ELIGIBILITY: artists of all disciplines may apply, with no age limit; submission is free of charge. The residency takes place exclusively online, and selected fellows agree to be available online during the four weeks. TIMELINE: the open call runs from July 7 to August 7, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Paid Research Assistant: AI and the War in Ukraine
item.description:
Organisation: Bogna Konior (NYU Shanghai)

Location: Remote-friendly.

Award: USD 18 per hour; 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule.

Duration: Ongoing part-time research assistantship; start as soon as possible.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

Bogna Konior, Assistant Professor of Media Theory at NYU Shanghai, is hiring a paid research assistant for a project on Ukraine and artificial intelligence. The work supports a forthcoming book with MIT Press, plus an independent scenario she is writing on the future of AI with the war as a lens. WHAT THE WORK LOOKS LIKE: going month-by-month through news archives (New York Times, Reuters, Financial Times, and specialist outlets), annotating and cataloguing coverage of AI, drones, and autonomous systems in the war, plus other developments she will direct you to; building and maintaining a structured Notion database covering chronology, companies, industries, and key actors; and organizing and editing her existing notes. WHO SHE IS LOOKING FOR: interest in and general knowledge of AI; prior research and annotation experience, careful and detail-oriented, no sloppy citations; comfortable with Notion and with AI tools; Ukrainian language skill a strong plus (not required); familiarity with AI, war, tech, and geopolitics news sources is great. Remote-friendly, start ASAP. She will also consider people with no prior experience, as well as current and previous students. TERMS: USD 18 per hour, 10-15 hours per week, flexible schedule. TO APPLY: email bogna@nyu.edu with a short introduction and background (link to website, socials, CV, LinkedIn, or portfolio if you have one); where you are based and your weekly availability; and one recent AI-related story, company, or development you find interesting and why (a vibe check). Bonus: a sample of past research or writing if you have it.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Alabama Historical Commission: Clotilda Memorial Public Art Commission
item.description:
Organisation: Alabama Historical Commission (with Forecast Public Art)

Location: Africatown, Mobile, Alabama, USA; open to artists internationally.

Award: USD 2,000,000 total project budget for a paid public art commission. No application fee.

Duration: Permanent public art commission

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

The Alabama Historical Commission, working with public-art consultancy Forecast Public Art, seeks proposals for a major memorial artwork honoring the survivors of the Clotilda, the last known ship to illegally traffic enslaved Africans to the United States (1860). The memorial will convey the survivors' lives and legacies and be developed through collaborative community engagement with historic Africatown, the community founded by Clotilda survivors, and the broader diaspora. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists of all disciplines, including time-based and media artists; individuals and teams; open internationally. BUDGET: USD 2,000,000 total project budget. DEADLINE: 9 August 2026, 11:59pm CST. Applications are handled through the CODAworx RFQ platform; find the Clotilda Memorial listing in the CODAworx opportunities index. item.title: AGI Governance Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Johns Hopkins University, School of Government and Policy (SOGP)

Location: Hopkins Bloomberg Center, Washington, D.C. (in-person only)

Award: Fully funded: travel, accommodation, and meals covered.

Duration: Three-week in-person intensive, 8-25 September 2026

Deadline: 30 Jul 2026

The AGI Governance Fellowship is the inaugural three-week intensive run by Johns Hopkins University's School of Government and Policy (SOGP) to develop the next generation of leaders in AI governance. COVERS: frontier AI legislation at state and federal levels; building societal resilience amid rapid capability growth; democratic practices in AGI alignment; and designing new governing institutions for the AGI era. WHO SHOULD APPLY: early-career researchers and practitioners (within roughly eight years of undergrad) with solid foundations in AI governance, including congressional staffers, AGI company employees, recent CS or policy PhDs, and nonprofit/civil society professionals; a postgraduate degree or equivalent experience is required. This is a capstone program for rising leaders, not an intro course. FUNDING: travel, accommodation, and meals are fully covered. DATES: 8-25 September 2026 at the Hopkins Bloomberg Center in Washington, D.C. (in-person only). DEADLINE: apply by 15 July 2026 for full consideration; rolling applications accepted. item.title: Prospect Art: NEW WORK Commission 2027 - "We Come in Pieces"
item.description:
Organisation: Prospect Art

Location: Remote / online. Open to visual artists worldwide; programs (ONE WORK, BROADCAST) run online.

Award: NEW WORK: USD 1,500 commission for one artist, plus a featured website section and social promotion. BROADCAST: USD 125 screening or speaking fee if selected. ONE WORK: a published article (exposure). NOTE: a USD 10 administrative fee applies to apply, with a no-questions-asked waiver available on request before the deadline. PayPal account required to receive funds.

Duration: The commissioned NEW WORK project should be completed within twelve months of selection.

Deadline: 16 Aug 2026

Prospect Art invites visual artists worldwide to apply for the 2027 NEW WORK commission, a USD 1,500 project grant supporting the creation of a new, conceptually driven work responding to the curatorial theme "We Come in Pieces," developed by 2026-2027 Curatorial Fellow Alma Sammel. Through a single application, artists are also considered for ONE WORK (an online series featuring an in-depth conversation centered on a single completed artwork) and BROADCAST (a curated online program of screenings, artist talks, and discussions). THEME: "We Come in Pieces" proposes the Alien as a method for shifting perspective, approaching alienness as a practice of estrangement to encounter the world beyond familiar human-centered narratives. It explores relationships between human and more-than-human worlds, speculative cosmologies, ecological systems, technologies, landscapes, histories, infrastructures, and nonhuman forms of intelligence (satellites and orbital debris, fungal networks, oceans, atmospheric systems, algorithmic intelligences), inviting works that displace the point of listening, seeing, sensing, or knowing. Research-based and conceptually driven practices are especially encouraged. WHAT'S OFFERED: NEW WORK, USD 1,500 to one artist plus a featured website section and social promotion (project completed within 12 months); ONE WORK, a published article and social promotion; BROADCAST, a USD 125 screening or speaking fee and inclusion in a themed program. ELIGIBILITY: professional visual artists working in any medium at any career stage, except those currently enrolled in undergraduate or graduate studio art programs; collectives may apply. Media welcomed include moving image, installation, sculpture, photography, performance, sound, digital media, social practice, and interdisciplinary forms. APPLICATION: submit via the official Google Form with contact information, a project description (250 words), a sketch/visualization (1-3 images), up to 3 links to completed work samples, a statement relating the project to the theme (200 words), a short bio and exhibition history (500 words), and a budget breakdown of the USD 1,500. A USD 10 administrative fee is paid via PayPal (include PayPal name and transaction ID); a fee waiver is available with no explanation by emailing submissions@prospectart.org before the deadline. Writers may email gioj@prospectart.org with samples. DEADLINE: 16 August 2026; results for the NEW WORK grant notified by end of September. INFO SESSION: 5 August 2026 at noon PST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: New Emergence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: New Emergence Art

Location: International; open worldwide. Shortlist shown in an online exhibition.

Award: Prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize cash award, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee.

Duration: One-off prize; shortlisted artists shown in an online exhibition 11 August to 7 September 2026

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

The New Emergence Art Prize 2026 is an international open call for emerging artists, presented by New Emergence Art. There is no set theme and work may be submitted in any medium. AWARDS: a prize fund worth over GBP 5,000 across nine awards supporting artists through cash prizes, artist materials, mentoring, and a residency, including a GBP 1,000 Grand Prize, a two-week NG Art Creative Residency in Provence, France (worth EUR 2,000), a GBP 300 Founder's Prize cash award, and materials or subscription awards from Staedtler UK, Rosemary & Co, GreatArt, Michael Harding, and The Art Ladder. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally to emerging artists; entrants may submit up to three original artworks that represent the quality, originality and direction of their practice. Final selections are made by an independent judging panel considering concept, execution, originality and impact. Shortlisted artists are featured in an online exhibition running 11 August to 7 September 2026. NOTE: there is a paid entry fee (amount set at the entry step). DEADLINE: 3 August 2026, 23:59 BST. Apply via https://www.newemergenceart.com/apply.

Open call details

item.title: Create NSW: Stories Matter NSW Literary Fellowships 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Create NSW (administered by Writing NSW)

Location: New South Wales, Australia; applicants must be NSW-based.

Award: AUD 50,000 per fellowship, 10 fellowships awarded (4 emerging, 3 mid-career, 3 established). No application fee.

Duration: 12-month fellowship

Deadline: 27 Jul 2026

Stories Matter is a new NSW literary fellowship program from Create NSW, administered by Writing NSW, awarding ten fellowships of AUD 50,000 each to support NSW-based writers to focus on a major new work over a 12-month period. The 2026 round funds four emerging, three mid-career, and three established writers working across prose fiction, poetry, illustration, graphic novels, and playwriting. ELIGIBILITY: writers must be based in New South Wales; open to writers at any career stage within the three tiers (not open to applicants outside NSW). No application fee. DEADLINE: 27 July 2026, 2:00pm AEST. Apply via the Create NSW grants portal.

Open call details

item.title: Heart2World Publishing: Heart2World Book Prize 2026 (Inaugural, Poetry)
item.description:
Organisation: Heart2World Publishing

Location: Nigeria; open to Nigerian writers currently residing in Nigeria.

Award: NGN 250,000 cash prize, a full publishing and marketing package (valued at approximately NGN 5 million), and 100 percent net royalties on the published collection. No entry or reading fee.

Duration: One-off prize; publication to follow

Deadline: 05 Aug 2026

The inaugural Heart2World Book Prize, from Heart2World Publishing, is a poetry prize for a full-length collection by a Nigerian writer. AWARD: NGN 250,000 cash prize, a full publishing and marketing package (valued at approximately NGN 5 million), and 100 percent net royalties on the published collection. ELIGIBILITY: Nigerian citizens currently residing in Nigeria; submit an unpublished poetry collection of 30 to 35 poems written in English. There is no entry or reading fee (verified on the funder page). DEADLINE: 5 August 2026. Apply via the prize page.

item.title: Fondazione Prada Film Fund 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Fondazione Prada

Location: Milan, Italy-based fund; open worldwide to independent producers and production companies.

Award: EUR 1.5 million annual endowment supporting up to 14 projects. Individual grant amounts are set by the Fondazione Prada selection committee (no fixed per-stage bands published). No application fee.

Duration: Feature film funding (development, production, or post-production)

Deadline: 06 Aug 2026

The Fondazione Prada Film Fund is an annual initiative supporting independent cinema, with an annual endowment of EUR 1.5 million backing up to 14 feature film projects across development, production, and post-production. The Fund imposes NO restrictions on geography, theme, genre, or language and does not participate as a co-producer, guaranteeing complete artistic freedom to filmmakers. It supports both established authors and new talents, as well as research projects. SELECTION is based exclusively on quality, originality, vision, and the strength and reliability of the applicant structure; projects are evaluated by independent readers and external consultants, with final selection and grant amounts determined by a committee appointed by Fondazione Prada. ELIGIBILITY: applications may be submitted only by a production company involved in the project (the applicant company remains solely responsible for meeting all requirements); open worldwide. DEADLINES (2026 call opened 30 June 2026): projects in production 6 August 2026; projects in development and post-production 10 September 2026 (this card uses the earliest deadline). Post-production projects may submit a rough cut until 20 November 2026. Selected projects announced February 2027. Application documents are on the Fondazione Prada Film Fund page; questions to filmfund@fondazioneprada.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Connections Through Culture Grants 2026 (British Council)
item.description:
Organisation: British Council

Location: International collaboration grant. Each application must involve at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they apply from throughout the project.

Award: Grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000. Free to apply.

Duration: Project-based; develop and deliver collaborative projects across a range of themes and art forms.

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

Connections Through Culture (CTC) grants are designed to enable meaningful partnerships, cross-cultural exchange and long-term collaboration between the UK and participating countries. In 2026, CTC incorporates the grants programmes formerly known as Biennials Connect (Visual Arts) and Architecture, Design and Fashion Biennials and Festivals Grants, and serves as the British Council's major Arts grants programme for 2026 (in place of International Collaboration Grants). It offers grants of between GBP 5,000 and GBP 15,000 to support creative practitioners, cultural professionals, artists, organisations, festivals, biennials, hubs and collectives to develop and deliver collaborative projects across a wide range of themes and art forms, including applied arts, architecture, curating, dance, design, digital, drawing, education, fashion, film, installation, interdisciplinary work, painting, performance, photography, printmaking, public art, research, sculpture, social practice, sound art, textiles, video, visual arts and writing. ELIGIBILITY: applications must involve collaboration between at least one UK-based partner and at least one partner based in an eligible participating country; each partner must be legally based in the country they are applying from at the time of application and throughout the project activity period. PARTICIPATING COUNTRIES: Albania, Argentina, Australia, Bangladesh, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Colombia, Mainland China, Egypt, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kosovo, Lebanon, Malaysia, Mexico, Montenegro, New Zealand, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Pakistan, Palestine, Peru, Philippines, Saudi Arabia, Serbia, South Africa, Tanzania, Thailand, Tunisia, Turkey, United Arab Emirates, UK, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Venezuela, and Vietnam. Eligible applicants include artists and creative practitioners, arts and cultural organisations, festivals and biennials, cultural professionals, creative hubs/networks/collectives, and organisations working across arts, culture and the creative sectors. Note: applications involving projects organised by or taking place as part of Visual Arts, Architecture, Design, Fashion and Craft festivals should be made by the festival or biennial organisation rather than by individual artists, designers or practitioners. No application fee. Deadline 12 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: TED & POSCA Global Artist Residency 2026: Visible Mending
item.description:
Organisation: TED & POSCA (curated by Fine Acts)

Location: Globally distributed; no single location or central studio. Selected artists work within their own communities, with projects unfolding in public space.

Award: Each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 (covering artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation), plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms.

Duration: One-year residency term; community-rooted public interventions developed in 2026 for implementation in 2027. The initiative runs for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

TED (the global platform for ideas and storytelling) and POSCA (a creative brand by uni Brands Corporation) are launching a new kind of artist residency: a globally distributed cohort of artists working across different geographies, cultures, and communities, united by a shared drive to shape public life through art. Fine Acts, the external curatorial partner selected by TED & POSCA, leads the curation, creative direction, and coordination. There is no single location and no central studio: selected artists work within their own communities, following a shared creative constraint (a unifying conceptual framework around repair, hope, and beauty), with projects unfolding in public space, community participation as a key part of the work, and process mattering as much as outcome. 2026 THEME, VISIBLE MENDING: inspired in part by Kintsugi (the Japanese practice of repairing broken ceramics with gold), the theme explores repair as a creative and civic act, inviting artists to consider how wounds, ruptures, and imperfections can become sites of transformation, and to make processes of repair visible through public interventions that spark reflection, dialogue, and collective imagination. In 2026, four artists will be selected to develop community-rooted public interventions to be implemented in 2027. AWARD: each selected artist receives a total of USD 15,000 to cover artist fees, production costs/community activation, and documentation, plus a production budget and tools from POSCA, creative direction and storytelling support, and visibility through TED and Fine Acts' global platforms. While the residency term is one year, the initiative will run for multiple years (2027, 2028, 2029), expanding the cohort year by year and building an evolving global archive of civic artistic interventions. Apply by 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wassaic Project 2027 Summer Exhibition Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: The Wassaic Project

Location: Wassaic, New York, United States (in and around historic Wassaic and Maxon Mills).

Award: A modest honorarium for all accepted artists (part of the organization's effort toward W.A.G.E. certification). Artists creating site-specific installations are also eligible for an Exhibitions Fellowship and a no-fee residency of 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027.

Duration: The 2027 Summer Exhibition runs free and open to the public every weekend, 1 May to 12 September 2027. Optional fellowship residency of 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027.

Deadline: 10 Aug 2026

The Wassaic Project is holding its annual Open Call for the 2027 Summer Exhibition, for artists of all mediums, including 2D work, sculpture, video, new media, site-specific installation, performance, text, poems, essays, and publication-specific work. Selected work is showcased alongside a diverse range of pieces and performances in and around historic Wassaic and Maxon Mills; the 2027 Summer Exhibition will be free and open to the public every weekend from 1 May to 12 September 2027. In an effort toward W.A.G.E. certification, all accepted artists receive a modest honorarium to participate. Artists interested in creating a site-specific installation are also eligible for an Exhibitions Fellowship to help realize their work; Fellows are offered a no-fee residency for 1-4 weeks in April or May 2027 (artists interested in making site-specific work should still apply regardless of whether they are interested in or able to be in residence). The Wassaic Project cultivates and supports community for emerging and professional contemporary artists, writers, and other creatives, with a residency program housed in historic landmark buildings that hosts 10 artists each month in the heart of a rural community. APPLICATION: requires application forms, a CV, and an artwork/portfolio; submitted via SlideRoom. Application fee: USD 25 (no participation fee or additional fees). Deadline 10 August 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Club Nothing: Community Music Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Nothing

Location: Anywhere in the world.

Award: USD 40,000 fund total; four individuals or collectives each receive USD 10,000 to produce a club event, series, or festival. No application fee.

Duration: Event, series, or festival to take place later in 2026; winners announced late August 2026.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

Club Nothing is a global events series and USD 40,000 fund from the tech brand Nothing, supporting organisers, artists, and collectives creating new music experiences. The fund will award four individuals or collectives USD 10,000 each to throw a club event, series, or festival later this year, backing disruptors bringing new sounds and experiences to their communities through nightlife anywhere in the world. Applications are judged by a panel including DJ and producer Manuka Honey (Marissa Malik), Nothing Global Senior Community Manager and Madruga Festival co-founder Erica Travis, Nothing Global Head of Communications Shavone Charles, Dazed Music Editor Solomon Pace-McCarrick, A&R and music-publishing founder James Supreme, and music-industry professional Layla Mustafa Rock. Applications close 9 August 2026; winners announced late August 2026. Learn more and apply at nothing.tech/club-nothing (details also posted at https://nothing.community/d/59214-want-10000-to-throw-a-party). See full terms at nothing.tech/club-nothing/terms-and-conditions.

item.title: Call to Artists: Walden Fire Station and Library (Calgary Arts Development)
item.description:
Organisation: Calgary Arts Development (CADA)

Location: Walden community, Calgary, Alberta, Canada. Open to local and national (Canadian) professional artists/artist teams.

Award: Total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes) for one selected artist/artist team. The artist fee for the total project is CAD 68,750 + GST. Budget breakdown can be adapted to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation that a minimum 20% of the budget go to the artist's fee; the total allocated budget will not be increased.

Duration: Concept and design from September 2026 to end of July 2027; fabrication and installation (Phase 2) from the second half of 2027 into 2028.

Deadline: 28 Jul 2026

Calgary Arts Development (CADA) is seeking an artist or artist team to join The City of Calgary's Walden Fire Station and Library project team to create permanent public artwork(s) for a combined fire station and library planned for the community of Walden. One artist/artist team will be selected with a total project budget of CAD 275,000 (exclusive of all applicable taxes); the opportunity is open to local and national professional artists/artist teams working in various media. ELIGIBILITY: local and national mid-career to late-career professional public artists and artist teams with a minimum of five years' previous experience creating artwork in the public realm; they must have at least three outdoor permanent public art installations, each with a lifespan of over 10 years and a minimum project budget of CAD 150,000 (see the Canada Council for the Arts definition of a professional artist). Walden does not currently have an active community association, so the project requires experience in artist-led community engagement and a strong understanding of alternative outreach. Artists must be available to meet the project timelines from September 2026 to the end of concept and design by July 2027; fabrication and installation take place in Phase 2, starting in the second half of 2027 and continuing into 2028. REWARD: total project budget CAD 275,000 (artist fee CAD 68,750 + GST); CADA is open to adapting the budget breakdown to the artist's approach, following CARFAC's recommendation of a minimum 20% to the artist's fee, though the total budget will not be increased. TIMELINE: applications open 16 June 2026 (9am MT); pre-recorded information session posted early July; application deadline 28 July 2026 (no applications accepted after 1pm MT); shortlisted interviews mid-September; results late September 2026. Eligibility questions: walden@calgaryartsdevelopment.com.

Open call details

item.title: Genesis Mission AI Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Horizon Institute for Public Service (with Renaissance Philanthropy, SeedAI, and Fulcrum Science)

Location: United States. Fellows are forward deployed to US Department of Energy national labs (initial partners: Idaho National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory). Remote or in-person; on-site travel at least once a quarter. Must be eligible to work in the US (no visa sponsorship; US green card holders eligible).

Award: Prorated total annual compensation of USD 200,000.

Duration: 9-12 months, starting fall 2026.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Horizon Institute for Public Service, Renaissance Philanthropy, SeedAI, and Fulcrum Science are partnering to launch a fellowship that embeds technical AI talent across the US Department of Energy national labs to advance the goals of the Genesis Mission, DOE's flagship science and technology initiative launched in late 2025. The Genesis Mission identifies 26 national science and technology challenges critical to US leadership and envisions a national platform connecting supercomputers, experimental facilities, AI systems, and unique datasets, with DOE's 17 national labs playing a key role. Genesis Mission AI Fellows are forward deployed to DOE national labs to bring frontier AI expertise to high-priority scientific challenges. As core members of the technical staff on a Genesis Mission project, fellows work alongside national lab scientists and partners and apply AI to national priorities, from building cutting-edge models of plasma dynamics in pursuit of controlled fusion to advancing state-of-the-art models for foundational materials discovery. Initial national lab partners include Idaho National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, and the Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory; additional partners may be announced later. COMPENSATION/DURATION: 9-12 months starting fall 2026, with a prorated total annual compensation of USD 200,000; fellows are expected to travel to their national lab site at least once a quarter but may work on-site if they prefer. ELIGIBILITY: competitive applicants should have at least 2 years of industry experience building AI models or agents (those with industry experience building AI applications or data infrastructure will also be considered); scientific domain expertise is not required, but any placement will involve some training and study in a specific scientific discipline or energy topic, supported by expert colleagues at the host institution. All fellows must be eligible to work in the United States for the duration of the placement; US employment visas cannot be sponsored, but US permanent residents (green card holders) are eligible. APPLICATION: initial form requires a CV or resume (max 2 pages) and short-answer questions on motivations and experiences (max 200 words); successful applicants complete interviews through partner organizations and national labs, and selected fellows are matched to partner labs by scientific domain and availability. The review uses evidence-based best practices including blind grading of application materials. TIMELINE: application deadline 31 July 2026; final fellow selection August-September 2026; expected start fall 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NEIU Art + Design Studio Art Residency 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Northeastern Illinois University (NEIU), Art + Design Department

Location: Salme Harju Steinberg Fine Arts Center, Northeastern Illinois University, Chicago, Illinois, United States. In-person; semester-long access to NEIU facilities.

Award: USD 800 honorarium, plus a semester of full access to NEIU Art + Design facilities specific to the project (Art + Tech, Ceramics, Drawing, Painting, Photography, Printmaking, Sculpture); supplies can be requested with guidance and approval from Studio Area personnel. Visiting Artists may also exhibit in a group exhibition in the NEIU Fine Arts Center Gallery.

Duration: One semester. Residency Session 1: 25 January - 14 May; Session 2: 30 August - 11 December.

Deadline: 09 Aug 2026

The NEIU Art + Design Studio Art Residency strives to create visible connections between Northeastern Illinois University's diverse population of art and design students and working artists who embody these identities, providing insights and inspiration through a dynamic exchange of ideas and techniques. Visiting Artists should demonstrate a commitment to their artistic practice and an interest in engaging with NEIU's students and creative community. Each Visiting Artist is awarded an honorarium and has full access for a semester to the NEIU Art + Design facilities specific to their project; over the residency they showcase their work in a public academic forum, lead a hands-on workshop, and participate in a Portfolio Review Day for NEIU's Art + Design students. FACILITIES (in the Salme Harju Steinberg Fine Arts Center) include a Design Studio with the largest collection of Riso drums at any Chicago university, a Fab Lab (FDM and resin 3D printers, large-format CNC router, vinyl cutters, laser cutters), a Ceramic Studio (throwing wheels, glaze room, electric and gas kilns, raku area), a Letterpress Studio (two Vandercook presses, metal and wood type), a Sculpture Studio (wood shop, clay modeling and spray painting areas), a Photography Studio (analog and digital, plus a lighting studio), and fully equipped Painting, Drawing, and Printmaking studios. BENEFITS: USD 800 honorarium; facility access based on project focus (Art + Tech, Ceramics, Drawing, Painting, Photography, Printmaking, Sculpture), with supplies requestable subject to approval; and the opportunity to exhibit in a group exhibition in the NEIU Fine Arts Center Gallery. PARTICIPATION: lead a 2-hour hands-on workshop for 15-20 students on a topic relevant to the project; give an hour-long artist talk with Q&A; and serve as a guest panelist for a day-long portfolio review. KEY DATES: open call 2 June - 9 August; emailed acceptances in October; Residency Session 1 runs 25 January - 14 May and Session 2 runs 30 August - 11 December. Apply via the JotForm application. Deadline 9 August 2026.

Open call details
